response:
the error occurs because your code references the .NET Framework version of WPF which is not available in .NET 7 on any platform including Windows.

you need to ensure that your shared project or class library does not directly depend on the WPF assembly by using conditional compilation symbols to separate the Windows specific logic from the cross platform code.

cousin applications often face similar issues when mixing legacy frameworks with modern cross platform targets so you must isolate the UI rendering logic behind an interface that both WPF and Cocoa can implement.

create a separate Windows specific project that references the WPF packages and expose a factory method in your shared code that only loads this project when running on Windows using platform detection.

this approach allows your main .NET 7 application to remain fully cross platform while still utilizing native WPF controls on Windows without triggering the missing assembly error.
